summary: Iteration 22 — Phase 2 Refer a Friend. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases pass (TestReferMe + TestSignupRefBehavior + TestVerifyOtpReferralCompletion + TestReferList + TestReferMeStatsReflectActivity). Verified: (a) /refer/me returns code+link+reward(25)+total/successful/pending/credits_earned; (b) backfill creates a referral_code for legacy users; (c) signup with valid ref → pending referrals row + user.referred_by set; (d) invalid ref → silent (no row); (e) self-referral (same email as referrer) silently dropped; (f) ref ignored for professional & employer signups (employer is 400-blocked entirely); (g) student first-time verify-otp flips pending→successful, awards +25 credits to referrer, writes referral_reward txn, sends in-app notification; (h) welcome_bonus=100 contract preserved both with and without ref; (i) /refer/list returns masked emails ('xx***@domain'), newest-first ordering, reward_credits=25. Frontend: One CRITICAL bug found and fixed by testing agent — /signup screen had a red-screen ReferenceError because `useLocalSearchParams` was not imported in /app/frontend/app/signup.tsx (the symbol was used on line 25 but only useRouter was imported on line 3). Patched the import. After fix verified: /signup?ref=USERECE1AD84 renders the green referral banner with the code + 'earn 25 Credits' copy; submitting the form sends `"ref":"USERECE1AD84"` in the POST body (network-capture confirmed); /signup with no query param shows no banner and signup still works; /student/refer renders with code USERECE1AD84, copy-code & copy-link buttons, all 7 share tiles (WhatsApp/Telegram/Facebook/Instagram/Gmail/Copy/More), 4 stat boxes (total/successful/pending/earned); Profile menu → 'Refer a Friend' navigates to /student/refer (no longer shows 'Coming Soon'); bottom tab bar still shows exactly 5 tabs (Home/Jobs/Interviews/LeadBoard/Profile) — refer is hidden via href:null.

ui_bugs.issue: MISSING IMPORT (fixed by testing agent): `useLocalSearchParams` was used on line 25 but only `useRouter` was imported on line 3 from expo-router, causing a hard red-screen 'ReferenceError: useLocalSearchParams is not defined' on EVERY visit to /signup (both with and without ?ref=). Fix applied: `import { useRouter, useLocalSearchParams } from "expo-router";`

issues: The ref banner (lines 71-78) has no testID. Adding `testID="signup-ref-banner"` to the banner View would improve testability (not blocking — verified text content instead).

react_native_issues.issue: Recurring web log 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' continues to appear in expo.err.log across many shared components (ProfileMenuSheet, Card, etc.). Not introduced by this iteration but still present. Reference: /app/frontend/src/components/ProfileMenuSheet.tsx:94-98 and others.

action_items: (DONE by testing agent) Added missing `useLocalSearchParams` import in /app/frontend/app/signup.tsx — main agent should keep this fix and consider adding a lint rule / TS strict-mode check so undefined symbols are caught at build time.
(Nice-to-have) Add `testID="signup-ref-banner"` to the ref-banner View in signup.tsx (lines 71-78) for stronger automated checks.
(Tech debt — not blocking) Migrate `shadow*` style props to `boxShadow` across shared components to silence RN-web deprecation warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /refer/me + /refer/list correctly use `{_id:0}` projections — no ObjectId leak.
Backend correctly enforces `body.role == "student"` before lookup → professional & employer refs are dropped without DB writes.
Self-referral guard exists at both signup (line 636) and verify-otp (line 750) — defense in depth is good.

Referrer credit-award atomicity: $inc + transactions.insert + referrals.update + push_notification are sequential, not transactional. In MongoDB without a session, a crash between the credit $inc and the referrals update could leave a 'paid but still pending' state — minor risk but worth noting. Consider wrapping in a Motor session transaction.
/refer/list masks email correctly (first 2 chars + ***@domain) and sorts created_at desc.
Frontend signup.tsx had a hard red-screen because of a missing import — caught by automated UI test. Suggests build-time type-checking is bypassed or not run.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/signup.tsx (1-line fix: added `useLocalSearchParams` to the expo-router import on line 3)

success_rate.frontend: 100% after the 1-line import fix to signup.tsx

seed_data_creation: None permanent. Tests create temporary users via /auth/signup with `referme.io` / `acmecorp.io` test domains — not cleaned up but isolated by uuid-prefixed emails. The self-referral test temporarily mutates the student fixture's email and restores it in a try/finally.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 Refer-a-Friend is fully verified end-to-end. Backend referral flow (pending→successful, +25 credits, txn, notification, masked /refer/list, /refer/me backfill) is tested in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration22_refer_a_friend.py. Frontend signup.tsx had a missing import (useLocalSearchParams) which has been fixed by the testing agent — verify this remains in place if signup.tsx is touched again. Anti-fraud guards (self-referral, non-student-role, invalid code) all behave correctly.
rca_of_issue: Frontend bug RCA: /app/frontend/app/signup.tsx line 25 used `useLocalSearchParams<{ ref?: string }>()` but the import statement on line 3 (`import { useRouter } from "expo-router";`) did NOT include `useLocalSearchParams`. Result: hard ReferenceError on every render of /signup, blocking the entire signup flow including the new ?ref banner. Reproduction: open /signup or /signup?ref=ANY in browser → red-screen 'ReferenceError: useLocalSearchParams is not defined'. Mitigation applied: appended `useLocalSearchParams` to the expo-router import. After a Metro re-bundle (sudo supervisorctl restart expo), the page renders cleanly and ref param is parsed + sent in POST body.
